kefalonian-tragio-with-thick-macaroni-ar.name: Kefalonian Tragio With Sick Macaroni
instructions: We prefer a piece of goat to neck and ask the button to clean it up and cut it into relations.
Ask the butcher and a few goat bones because this will enhance the flavor of the sauce.
Allow it to come to room temperature.
Dry very well with abstract paper and add some salt and some pepper and mix to go everywhere.

kefir-rice-pudding-lezzet.name: Kefir Rice Pudding
instructions: 1. The pith will be lightly arranged and uncensored.
2. Open with milk until a glass of starch.
3. Kefir, cream, milk and powder sugar in a pot.
4. Boil by mixing intermediates.
5. Start boiling milk cook and cook push from the space until adding starch and get mixed until it gets absorbed.
6. Share the service bowls.
7. Take the waittip refrigerator until it comes to the room temperature and relax for two hours.
8. Bun, frambuaz and mint.
9. Serve with ice cream.

kerris-holiday-punch.name: Kerri's Holiday Punch
instructions: In a large punch bowl combine Southern Comfort liqueur, lemon juice, lemonade concentrate, and orange juice concentrate.
Stir in lemon-lime soda and add a few drops red food coloring.
Float orange and lemon slices for garnish.

kimchi-miso-dressing.name: Kimchi Miso Dressing
instructions: Combine kimchi, miso, tofu, oil, and vinegar in a blender and purée until very smooth; season with salt. Refrigerate until ready to use. Dressing may thicken as it sits; before serving, thin with water as desired.
Do Ahead: Dressing can be made 4 days ahead; store in an airtight container and chill.

kimchi-quesadillas.name: Kimchi Quesadillas

ingredients.item: Coarsely grated oaxaca cheese or low-moisture mozzarella

instructions: Heat 1 Tbsp. unsalted butter in a small skillet over medium. Cook 1 cup chopped drained kimchi and ½ tsp. sugar, stirring occasionally, until it darkens and dries out a bit, about 5 minutes. Add 1 tsp. soy sauce; cook, stirring, 1 minute. Remove from heat.
Place ½ cup sour cream and 2 Tbsp. kimchi juice in a small bowl and squeeze in juice from 1 lime wedge; mix well. Stir in a pinch of garlic salt; set seasoned sour cream aside.

Heat a dry large nonstick skillet over medium. Lay a 10"–12"-diameter flour tortilla in pan and top one side with ½ cup coarsely grated Oaxaca cheese or low-moisture mozzarella, then half of kimchi mixture. Top with another ½ cup coarsely grated Oaxaca cheese or low-moisture mozzarella and a pinch of garlic salt. Fold tortilla up and over filling to make a half-moon shape. Cook, turning halfway through, until tortilla is golden and cheese is melted, about 5 minutes total. Transfer to a plate and sprinkle with thinly sliced scallion greens. Repeat to make 1 more quesadilla.
Serve quesadillas with reserved seasoned sour cream and remaining 3 lime wedges for squeezing over.

kiwi-cream-teleculinaria.name: Kiwi Cream
instructions: Peel the kiwis, cut them roughly and shred them until you get a mashed potatoes. Reserve.
Beat the cream with the sugar until you get a very firm whipped cream. Add vanilla, lemon shaving to taste and coconut milk, always beating.

In individual bowls, place a layer of roughly broken toothpicks. Cover it with the kiwi mashed and then the cream. Repeat the layers until the ingredients are gone. Take it to the cold until it serves.
